analysis.name: Abayomi Afe
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Afe conducted incredibly engaging lectures drawing from real world experiences in the field to prepare us for scenarios we will likely encounter upon graduation and entering the public health space. I gleaned so much from this course and grew as a health professional in a domain I had least expected as a result of taking this class.

There was a language barrier that interfered with my understanding of his assignment prompts. His use of certain words have different meanings in the English language, so interpretation of assignment prompts and discussion questions are very difficult. He also has difficulty explaining assignments in detail.

Dr. Afe is very knowledgeable in Issues and Concepts in Maternal and Child Health and explains complex information in a clear, relatable way. He uses strong data to discuss current global and U.S. trends and encourages students to be part of positive, impactful change.

Dr. Afe was an incredible professor who was understanding and compassionate, yet challenging at the same time. He was one of the most incredible professor I have had at Purdue Global and throughout my higher education. I highly recommend taking any course he is teaching.
Dr. Afe was very knowledgeable, professional, and passionate about the subject. I thoroughly enjoyed the course.

analysis.name: Yasmeen Aleem
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I liked her class overall, it was not bad as an introduction to psychology. Some of the course material relied too much on the textbook and was not very flexible though.
She was so rude when she said "I don't know what you were writing" publicly in class, when I asked her about my grade. Hated it.

I did not care for this professor because she always told her class to reach out if they needed help or had questions. Most of the time she never bothered to reply to emails I sent. She is a lovely person and great with the seminars, but the lack of communication turned me away from this professor altogether.
I really enjoyed the class. She was very interactive in seminar. Course has 4 assignments that range from papers to a video recording. She gave great feedback. Be prepared to read.

analysis.name: Adrian Johnson
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Johnson was knowledgeable and seminars were interesting. I took him in Jan. 2024 at the beginning of my degree program. There is SO MUCH READING. Stay on top of it, or you'll get behind. I think this class had the most reading of my entire Healthcare Admin program. I learned so much in his class and enjoyed it very much.

Adrian Johnson was a nice, personable instructor. He kept the seminars interesting and gave us good information. He does give a LOT of reading though, so be prepared for that. He's not an easy grader. Pay close attention to the rubrics and you'll be fine. He doesn't return email the fastest either. If you get stuck, use the student center.

Zoom classes and presentations are the bare minimum/nothing is really taught
Talks so much without ever actually saying anything
Spends half the class complaining about slackers yet takes weeks to put in grades while having barely any assignments
Doesn't provide USEFUL help/options to students who actually want to put in the work and time to pass

analysis.name: Deborah Adell
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Working with Dr. Adell has been an absolute nightmare. She is unfair in her grading, taking off points that never in my entire career at Purdue Global have I experienced. She is extremely rude, and insists on meeting via Google Chat, however she then won't honor what she says while meeting with her. She is very unhelpful. She is terrible.

Switch professors if you can. She is extremely rude. She is condescending. Will tell you one thing then take points off for doing that. She will also state that you went over it already and has never done that. She told the class she won't review how she wants the sources because we are far enough into the program that she shouldn't have to do it.

Poor feedback, grades come in late. does not provide constructive criticism right away on the offense. Doesn't ever show her face, is condescending and dismissive, immediately threatens with disciplinary action and academic review. Does not allow room to ask questions in zoomclass, exits the class mid sentence of the student asking.
Lectures were unclear and not pertinent to the content of the week. Quizzes were nearly impossible to study for. She took off points for the slightest mistake. I learned nothing from her.

Wow...where do we even start? This woman is the definition of "eating their young" and it makes for a very disheartening learning experience. The course material itself is OK, but the prof is...mean. She unreasonably picks on students and seems to just have a general disdain toward anyone younger than herself. Also quite illiterate.

analysis.name: Brenda Harper
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I had Dr. Harper twice & I was extremely frustrated both times. Unfortunately, I believe she is the only one teaching MT499 (capstone), so if you intend to graduate, you're stuck. My biggest issues with her are her passive-aggressive comments when students ask questions, her long, rambling, repetitive posts,  & her wildly inconsistent directions.

Brenda is the worst professor ever. She puts students on blast to the whole class for asking questions and she argues with students in seminar. One time she randomly started crying in seminar and by the end of it she was berating a student for asking questions and stating his opinion that she was contradicting herself. AVOID HER CLASSES.

I have never had a more hostile and rude teacher. She spends a majority of seminars yelling about issues she's had with past groups and is disorganized and contradicts herself in seminars, announcements, and emails. The blatant disrespect that she displays is wild. She makes up assignments and badges that do not exist.

As a capstone class, the lack of discussion and usefulness of attending classes is extremely disappointing. Each class she contradicts herself continuously in instructions. No actual course information is discussed just long rants about how she hates having tough love. Professor does not provide further development that companies are looking for.

This professor is rude and contradictive in her lessons. She leaves early and I leave more confused. She offers her personal cell phone number in case you need help only to be rude and tell you you should already know when you reach out. She is all over the place in general. Save yourself the stress.

analysis.name: Beatrice Brown
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Brown is an excellent, highly knowledgeable professor. Her grading is strict but always fair, and she pushes students to truly master the material. Some misunderstand her firmness, but she is genuinely supportive and committed to helping students succeed and grow. The best educator, hands down.

Dr. Brown was incredibly rude. She's a strict grader but that is never a negative from me, that's life. She, however, is one of the most condescending and unhelpful instructors I've ever encountered. She does the absolute bare minimum and does it begrudgingly. Avoid at all costs.

Dr. Brown was hands-down, the rudest and most unprofessional instructors I have personally had to ever deal with. She was standoffish even in the first ever “lecture”, then in following lectures she seemed like she was reading from some kind of AI generated script.

Dr. Brown is clear and organized. Follow APA formatting closely because she is a stickler for that. She doesn't control the quizzes or exams, it would be helpful if she reviewed the questions with the class. The quizzes and tests are challenging, but if you follow the rubric and meet the requirements for the other assignments, you'll do well.

Worst Professor Ever!

She has no mercy, no flexibility, and cares more about APA formatting than actual learning. A patient won't refuse my care over a citation error, but she acts like it's life or death. No compassion, harsh grading, and zero willingness to help. Save yourself the stress and take someone else!

analysis.name: Barry Regan
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Really great seminars! Fun, engaging, not a heavy course load just know your APA and you'll do fine! Pro tip, actually engage in the seminars (hop on the mic and chat) it'll make the class a lot more fun!
Great professor works with the student!

He can be a tough grader, really picking about formatting and grammer. He cares a lot so make sure to reach out to him, he shows grace. If you use a lot of direct quotes and definitions from the textbook you will do fine. He manily gives instructions through pre recorded video just be prepared to watch a.

Dr. Barry was pretty easygoing, and enjoyable during seminars. The assignments were creative and not too difficult as long as you manage your time well. He gave many opportunities to turn in late work and an alternative assignment if you missed the seminar. Also, he made himself available through text, e-mail, and phone. Overall, great instructor.

analysis.name: Broderick Martinez
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Fair in grading, yet a very demanding and blunt professor. Learned a lot, but it wasn't easy. If you want a class that really teaches you the material, then this is it. You need to be ready to put in 200% though.

During seminar, if you ask questions or don't understand what he's saying, he'll make you feel dumb. He'll just say, "I already told you....." This class has tons of homework too. He was upfront in the first week by telling us that if we feel like this class is too much, we need to make a decision about staying in it or not. At least he was blunt.
Very demanding during lectures. Tough grader. WOULD AVOID!

analysis.name: Cassandra Tate
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: DO NOT TAKE A COURSE WITH HER. No matter how great you are on APA she will deduct mass points. We are not English students. She should not be teaching healthcare admin. I have never posted about a professor and am an A student but she was that bad. Seriously just don't do it.

RUN! Worse professor ever! Your GPA will drop, and she does not care about your topic or quality. I am not sure why she is not an English professor instead of teaching Health Science. Teaching is not her forte.

She will take so many points for APA format and in-text citations. She will deduct as many points as possible from your assignments and be strict on writing rules. Just re-register for class with another professor.

You better be a genius in APA format and submit every single paper to the writing center. She does not care about the quality of your paper, only that your paper is perfect APA format. Choose someone else!! She plummets GPA's!
If you can take someone else, do!

analysis.name: Timothy Carlin
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the best professors I've encountered. His meticulous approach and high expectations pushed me to work harder and pay close attention to detail.

Professor Carlin is knowledgeable, tough, fair, encouraging and most of all available to the student at a number of different times throughout the week. So there is really no excuse for not being successful in Professor Carlins' classes. I've had the pleasure of being one of his students several times through my education. Wish I could do it again.

Professor Carlin is one of the very best professors I've had. He is always helpful and provides good feedback. He is completely knowledgeable about the subject matter and will bend over backwards to help the student "get" the information being taught.

The quizzes are a bit difficult and he dose not give that much time to complete them like other professor do. However, he is understanding and allows us to go over the time as long as it is not an obsessive amount of time

analysis.name: Jonathan Cardew
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Cardew was highly supportive and professional. Always answered emails promptly, and grading was fair. Work was simple, and he explained expectations very clearly in Zoom classes.

Mr. Cardew was positive. He ran great online seminars, He engaged with all of his students. He didn't drone on in class. He responded promptly to any questions. Great at communication. His feedback was informative. He knows his subject very well. And he's kind. He is what a professor should be.
I loved professor Cardew, he was an excellent and caring teacher. Very attentive and engaging and a fair grader. I wish he taught more course would love to have him again.

Professor Cardew provides a great experience!  His seminars are informative and engaging, his assignments are interesting to do, and his rubrics are easy to follow.  He grades assignments directly from the rubric, and is very clear about what you're doing correctly, and what can use some improvement. I thoroughly enjoyed working with him.
Prof. Cardew was approachable and professional. He graded exactly to the rubrics provided and was forthright when defining expectations.

analysis.name: Carrie Stringham
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She is great expectations are clear. She comes off as very strict in the beginning but is very easy to work with. She makes sure expectations are clear. I absolutely hate group projects, but she graded it fairly and ensured everyone participated.
Overall is an alright professor. She just has a tendency to be very slow when talking in lectures and often puts us in little break rooms with other students (which no one likes).

The class is not difficult, but the professor is. She takes forever to grade assignments and when she does grade them, it's not always accurate. Death by powerpoint so be ready. Talks more about herself than class. Assignment statistics show only a few pass the class. If you miss one thing on rubric you get a zero. DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR!!!

The professor was very communicative and clear on expectations for assignments. Class sessions were always engaging and interesting. There is a group project in this course which was challenging but grading is fair.
Professor Stringham is awesome!

analysis.name: Jennifer Claire
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Claire is great, really a great professor and a kind person. Her expectations are clearly laid out and she doesn't waste your time or her own. Would gladly take more classes from her.

I have never had a professor quite like Prof Claire. One of the most compassionate humans I have ever met! As long as you are understanding the material and putting in the effort, you will most definitely receive an "A". She is an easy grader but gives feedback to help you along. 10/10 would recommend this professor!

All of her assignments are fairly easy. Mostly just 1-page papers. Most weeks each discussion is a condensed version of the actual assignment. The workload is very manageable. No tests, just discussion posts/replies, and 6 assignments.
Professor Claire was great! She graded assignments in a timely manner and sometimes the same day.
Professor Claire is one of the best professors I have had at Purdue University.  She is clear, concise and hilarious!

analysis.name: Elliot Crozat
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very Simple, fair grader, very good at teaching the subject and letting you form your own opinions without giving his.  He uses all of the time for seminars, will go up until the final few minutes.  I would absolutely learn from him again.
No communication, very slow grading, harsh grading with no explanation.  I would avoid at all costs.

Dr. Crozat was a good instructor. The class was basically introing philosophy for the first few weeks then switched to more specific topics. It seemed overwhelming at first but once you figured out how he wanted you to interact and answer the topics it was much easier. Seminars are not very interactive but does pause at times to answer chat.

analysis.name: Daniel Dayton
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr Dayton has been one of my worst professors and harshest graders. He grades on very obscure things like capitalization of titles in APA, and doesn't provide adequate feedback to allow me to correct the mistakes in the future. This was a very stressful class and he did nothing to help me succeed. Not recommended.

This was a super stressful class, very demanding with needless extra assignments that didn't fully make sense - grade style was awful and rubric mentioned ensuring non offensive and inclusive language... University papers do not include offensive language at a graduate level. Very demeaning grading and his responses. Got an A, but he was the worst.

This is literally the worst professor I've had in my entire academic career. The man spends most of his lectures talking about himself while sitting in front of a wall of his accolades. I will not speak for others, but I do not care about what awards you have won; I care about the content that I am paying for. Very demeaning.

Students beware. Professor is pompous and egotistical. He knows his material and is very knowledgeable - but he is not a good educator. Long-winded and inconsistent with his grading. I believe they teach to feel important and they respond to assignment/discussions with copy paste sections of their published works and dissertations.

Only reason I got an A was because I taught myself. This professor is egotistical and full of himself. Terrible. He does not respond to discussions with teachable material but with his own work like showing off. He needs to be fired or taken down a few notches

analysis.name: Reginald Doctor
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment:
This is the worst professor I've ever encountered.. He's focused on English composition and not HRM. All he cares about is APA writing BUT this is not an APA class.  He will mention Turnitin 1000 times and call you out for cheating when it comes back with a higher score.
He doesn't care about the content of your work, just your Turnitin score

He's more strict on APA guidelines than any professor I've ever had. After fixing what he claims is wrong, he finds something new, never mentioned prior, next week. Grades based on political opinions over content. Lectures are dull and uninformative. Class is all  essays, so good luck maintaining a good grade, when rules change each week. Drop it.

He spoke very little about HR Management and spent most of the time discussing APA formatting, making it feel like a writing class. He seemed quite strict. After the seminar, I checked online and found that other responses matched my impressions. I called my advisor the next day to drop the course and requested a new professor.

Professor is ineffective due to overemphasis on personal monologue than SME. The tendency leads to disengaged students, missed learning opportunities, and frustration. The focus on self-promotion over learning likely results in a low rating for effectiveness, as students expect more direct instruction and engagement with actual material.

Reginald Doctor says that everything has to be APA format but has a different meaning for APA. APA 7 is the current set standard and he does not grade it as APA 7. Additionally when you ask him questions or to clarify he answers back that he doesn't understand the question or that he can't clarify more. Demotivates Students.

analysis.name: Tina Evans
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This Professor knows her course must be completed to move forward. No seminar or office hours. Does not follow the rubric or her  suggestions. Thinks everything is AI, grades extremely low. She expects the worst from everyone and delivers the worst back. Uses her tagline “you have some good work here do your very best next week”. After failing you.

Never take a course with Dr. Evans. I truly believe that she loves to fail students for fun. She will report you for using AI just for spelling your name correctly. She shows that she has favorite students and she hates the rest of us. This is the worst professor/teacher I have ever had in my life. The devil was nicer than her.

Dr Evans should not be allowed to teach any class because she doesn't teach. She tries to find reasons to fail students who reach out for help (which is by email only) I am halfway through her course, paying a ton of money to teach myself. If at all possible do not sign up for any class taught by this woman.

Dr. Evans gives little feedback after grading. "Do your very best" is not feedback. It's hard to know what you're doing wrong because she never tells you. She also thinks everything is AI generated, despite the fact that many of her own discussion prompts are clearly AI generated.

A professor with little experience in health care administration that does not follow her grading criteria or rubrics. It should be a mandatory of professor to have experience in the career field that they teach. You Google her name and there is no significant health care administration accomplishments

analysis.name: Eugene Gotwalt
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I took Microeconomics (BU224) with Professor Gotwalt. He does not let you call him anything except Professor. He has a very rambling and unusual lecture style and veers way off topic but he does get to the topic at hand eventually. He lets his political leanings show which is a distraction. I did learn a lot from this class but it was tough.

This professor is good in seminars. However he gives conflicting feedback on homework and will not clarify his feedback. He docs points with no explanation on how you can improve. He does not help if you reach out to him by email for clarification.

Dr. Gotwalt is a straight shooter. He is a published and respected instructor that knows the materials. The lectures are amazing and he is very engaging with any questions. He took some getting use to but it paid off. He will review the assigned unit homework so that you know what he expects. Easy grader if you put in the work and do it.

analysis.name: Darrell Evans
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I have taken 4 classes at PG and this professor is hands down the best. He is very accessible outside the classroom and will make adjustments based on your availability. My first child was born while I was still in term and not only did he congratulate me he gave me a generous extension that was much needed. He talks to you like a human should!

Professor Evans is outstanding in every aspect. He provides valuable feedback, sets clear expectations, and is easily accessible. His intelligence and understanding shine as he presents interesting and relevant content. Professor Evans is truly exceptional, and I highly recommend him for an enriching educational experience.
Best professor at Purdue, no other instructor was this way to speak to if you had concerns. Professor Evans does everything he can to help you get through.

analysis.name: Galia Fussell
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This professor belittles her students and often times gives unhelpful feedback. She spends most of the seminar either talking about how great she is or about how much she dislikes Ai. She is very unapproachable and demeaning. For your last project despite there being two options if you choose the PowerPoint she will take points off just because.

Does not actually teach, refers to online tutoring and writing center, never willing to help you directly. Does not follow rubric most of the time. Changes her mind constantly on what she is looking for, or she just misunderstands most of what is discussed. If you're hoping to feel inspired, you will NEVER get it from this educator.

I have never had such an issue with a professor. She constantly picks on people about APA format even if they are correct. If you do not cite anything her way she will knock so many points off of your assignment even for discussions. I am very frustrated having her because her class will tank your GPA.

Is too caught up in making her own rules for the class and trying to capture perceived evidence on students using AI to give proper feedback or advice on how to do better. Feels more like an authoritarian guard there to punish you then someone there to help you. Also extremely rude, many examples to list which I cannot fit.

Other students likely complain about this professor because they are accustomed to mediocrity earning them a high grade. This professor demands more and it is about time we see more of that in higher education. This professor will challenge you, but if you work hard, it will pay off, you will learn a lot, and get outside your comfort zone.

analysis.name: Fred Sakon
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I am that weird guy who loves tough professors. I hate an easy A, and always go to the extreme for a good grade. I put in the work. But this guy is terrible. He thinks he is being a "tough" professor, when in reality its just lazy and terribly inconsistent. Usually the tough professors are the ones that care the most - don't expect that here.

Prof. Sakon clearly cares about his students. While the class is largely self-taught, it is evident that he is invested in your success. He grades quickly and is always quick to respond to any questions. But be warned: You will most likely require many outside resources to succeed, especially if you struggle with math.

I did not know how discrete math would be of any use to me in IT but now I know. This professor had a lot of good examples of how the math and IT relate. He also pays attention to what you say in discussion so be careful with that.

Confusing, isn't clear on how he wants specific assignments done. I am a grammar freak and get easily annoyed with incorrect grammar, and somehow I have been getting marked down for grammar on my math assignments. One week we had a substitute teacher, and she explained things so much better. Professor Sakon just doesn't make sense when he teaches.

Students either did extremely well in the class or did really bad. I found that if I put in the time, effort, and work, it makes passing the class easier. He holds students accountable in discussion boards by ensuring every aspect of the question is answered. When I encountered issues in homework, he was extremely responsive and helpful via email.

analysis.name: Jeffrey Gulley
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I have had Professor for 3 classes and he has been my favorite Professor in my entire college journey. His lectures are informative and funny and he gives very clear and fair feedback on assignments. He is very active on the discussion board and responds quickly to emails. I would HIGHLY recommend taking any class with him leading it.

I have had Professor Gulley for two classes now. When I decided on the legal profession, I thought it would be challenging. Not at all! Professor Gulley keeps everyone engaged in his lectures and provides great feedback on assignments. He does not expect perfection, only effort.
Awesome professor! I had him for two terms and loved the classes. He grades fairly and gives great feedback. Seminars are hilarious and informative!
He is a great professor I was so happy to have him twice as my professor I really hope I have him one last time before I graduate
Gives amazing feedback is fair and understanding. Easy to reach if you have questions.

analysis.name: Gabriel Smith
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very tough grader - completing an assignment just to check the boxes on the grading rubric isn't enough. I had to redo every assignment I submitted in this course because he would not grade them until I made (in my opinion) small/unnecessary revisions that did not impact the overall quality of the assignment

This was definitely a class where I had to keep an open mind and exercise patience. The submission (or, more accurately, resubmission) process was tedious to say the least. To make it worse, I didn't learn much from the instructor's feedback and I feel it was done mostly for his benefit. He did not seem outwardly rude though, just a bit pedantic.

When I get graded on my assignments he tells me what I have to correct for the next assignment. When I do I get more points taken off because I did what I was told to. I cannot hit my word count in assignments because I'm doing what I'm told. When I ask for help, he tells me to ask someone else for help. He doesn't know.
Does not give good feedback on assignments, takes off points for the smallest mistakes, and makes discussion board posts extremely long for no reason.

Not a harsh grader - just... unreasonable. Provides vague assignments and feedback that is full of grammatical errors. Without a DOUBT, Professor G. Smith is the most inflexible arrogant instructor I have worked with at this University.

analysis.name: Rachel Gallardo
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Absolutely the worst I've ever had. She ruthlessly tears apart every assignment over APA formatting, ignoring true learning. With Covid-era University of Phoenix degrees, she lacks academic rigor. She couldn't hack it in the real world and should stick to her podcast. She has no place in teaching. Save yourself the stress, avoid her class.

Great teacher! Be ready to defend any AI use, even Grammarly. She wants a clear explanation of how you integrate it into your writing process. This includes why you used it, what specific features, and how it helped refine your work while ensuring the ideas remain your own. Transparency is key!

You will be accused of using AI if Grammarly is installed on your computer even the free version. Feedback is also vague and if you check every item off the rubric you will still receive a not so great grade.

analysis.name: Miriam  Gough
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I'm taking TH230 with Prof. Gough. The grading has been unclear, and despite my substantial efforts, I face frequent point deductions. The rubrics and materials are complex, making it hard to understand expectations. This is my first challenging course at Purdue, which is disappointing given my previous positive experiences. I wouldn't recommend it
Look, I get it, I didn't use the paragraph from the text book for that particular portion of the paper. But giving me that grade? Yeah right
A lot of work but follow the rubric and you'll be fine.

analysis.name: Heith Hennel
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Quite literally says everything is AI. Very annoying.

I didn't have any gripes with this professor. A lot of writing, but its a policy class. I have experience in this field so it wasn't very difficult. I cant speak for other students, but i did not have a bad time in this class.
3000 word minimum to pass, per assignment. Absurd amount of work. Atleast the grading is quick.

analysis.name: Hoijin Jung
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: When she critiques your work she draws on your drawings... i spend half an hour just erasing her sketches on my work. She also like laughs at your questions sometimes? very strange very annoying would not recommend her. Way too hard of a grader for an entry level class.

She's very passionate about the subject but was very difficult when it came to grading. Additionally, she didn't want to round my grade up by less than 1 percentage point to give me a grade bump. I showed up to every one of her three hour classes and was an active participant. Also the class was three hours long and definitely dragged at times
had her for intro drawing and ad265, would not recommend for drawing but was a great printmaking instructor. super knowledgeable and eager to help.

analysis.name: Holly Parker
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I will admit, Holly Parker made me feel uncomfortable, but in a good way! Imagine suddenly being face-to-face with a classmate you've never seen before, talking on mike about things that are bothering you, and then having to give advice to the other person about how they can overcome those issues!

At first I wasn't a huge fan she doesn't like when you use the chat without her permission for lectures yet you have to have a certain amount of responses. Her bird will make any animal in your house freak out because he yells during the entire lecture. Test are mostly easy because you can use the textbook. Good luck it was hard and fun all in 1.

Professor Parker was amazing! Her ultimate goal is for you to pass! She was a sweet lady, accepted late work (on some things) and is really passionate about teaching. I had her for HS111 and HS116 and passed both classes with flying colors. She allows you to use notes and textbooks on quizzes and no cumulative final.

analysis.name: Monica Hubler
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Accounting I is the class SImply a bad professor who floods you with work but has very short lectures which makes no sense. I truly hates the entire class .
She picks and chooses what to read or spend time grading. not understanding at all.
Dr. Hubler is an easygoing Professor. She wants you to succeed and is available to answer questions or walk you through labs. Her classes were fun and informative.

analysis.name: Heather Valdes
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Grading seemed random, no clear reason for points taken and feedback was never clear. Instead of giving feedback that could help you understand what you were doing wrong or what she was looking for in the assignment it would be questions like "is this correct?" "why would you do it like that?". I don't know, thats why I'm taking this class!

Gave me the same percentage on every assignment, only a 1 to 2-point difference, if any. On one of my close-to-final assignments, she gave me a D because I used wiki as a secondary source & claimed that I did not use Westlaw when I did. She knew this would drop my GPA. Yes I got an A- but that A- made all the difference in my 4.0 streak.

Professor Valdes was responsive when I had questions and got back to me via email in a timely manner. Her seminars were helpful and on topic and added to the course reading and assignments each week. She was quick to pause and interact with students, give feedback, answer questions, and expand upon assignments in seminars.

Professor Valdes was my business law professor. Her lectures were so much fun because she was very passionate about teaching everyone. Highly recommend taking her for a law class at purdue global. LS311 Business Law had many papers as well as lots of textbook reading but the class was great!
Useless feedback, slow to respond, terrible communication, passive aggressive. 0/10 would not take a class from her again even if it cost me the opportunity to get a degree.

analysis.name: Jennifer Haber
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: So disrespectful!!!! Very vague with her instructions. Avoid at all cost!! She is the absolute worst professor I've ever had. Purdue global should do an evaluation on her.
Do not take this professor. Clearly cannot differentiate between things. Wouldnt let me pass a module because I had four paragraphs, not three when the essay requirements said to write four.

The rubrics are up for interpretation and if you go exactly according to the rubric, still don't expect to get a perfect grade because she takes her own interpretation of what the rubrics mean over what they actually say. When you try to address a problem or concern with her, you're met with belittlement instead of actual help.

Very narrow with her feedback on assignments as well as always adding improvement(s) that are added already into the assignment. Also definitely engage like crazy during the seminars. If you don't it may hurt your grade.
You never can end up with a 100%. She is always pointing out what needs to be improved as well as engage as much as you can during the seminars.

James Taylor was the best professor I had. He was humourism and engaging. He graded my papers with a fair outlook and I felt there was no bias for views. All he asks for is a effort and if it shows you will grade well.
If you do not write to his ideology you will fail his class. He will present multiple ethical theories but if you use anything outside of his beliefs be prepared to fail.

Doctor Taylor does not offer validation. However, he does provide his students with an opportunity to learn ethics and further develop their critical thinking skills. He is a tough grader, but his critiques are supplemented with constructive feedback, which enables the students to reach their fullest academic potential.

Fantastic professor, but be ready to write. A lot of discussion posts, but just be on top of everything and you'll pass easily. Hard to fail this class
Prof Waldow is a fantastic professor. He is energetic, caring, and accessible.

Prof W is one of the best teachers at PG. He's easy-going and so fun. Loves participation in seminars but he's so high energy that you want to participate and have fun. Easy grader as long as you do the work and show you are trying. Great for feedback and if any extra help is needed he's very responsible. Homework for this class is manageable.

Professor Waldo is high energy, accesible at all times, provides good feedback, responds quickly, and has a good sense of humor. He cares about his students and is not trying to be a roadblock to your success. He does expect lots of interaction during seminar, but hes engaging so its not too difficult to do. Would highly recommend. He does

review_highlights.comment: Everything they say about this professor is true. She criticizes all students during lectures about AI/plagiarism but contradicts herself by using the same tools to respond to posts. She also gives no clear and concise guidelines about assignments and no positive feedback how to improve.

Dr. Phillips seemed somewhat knowledgeable. However, I was appalled by her teaching style. Rude and combative with students. No feedback on assignments, either good or constructive. Her responses to discussion boards were canned and not relevant.  Stressed citing but criticized class for over-citing. Terrible teacher for a capstone class.

All reviews about Jodee across various colleges are true. I came in hoping the reviews were wrong, but unfortunately, they weren't. If you can, find another professor. Grading is unclear, rubrics aren't followed, and seminar time is wasted every week talking about AI/plagiarism, even though she admits using the same tools she lectures about.

Rubrics don't provide all necessary information. You will miss points on small petty things. Do not take this professor. I've maintained a 3.9 GPA, and this is easily the worst professor I've had here.

review_highlights.comment: Would not recommend this professor. She is a very hard grader, took off points nearly every discussion post and assignment for "writing issues," but did not point out what those issues were. If you emailed her with questions about an assignment, she would copy and paste the existing instructions instead of addressing the questions in your email.

Dr Pitts, was a very nice professor she was very understanding she helped with me even after classes. Attendance is very important to her, she might be hard but if you ask for help you can pass her class.

Dr. Pitts was helpful during my final term at PG and provided excellent feedback to enhance my writing as I prepared for graduate school. She also provided me with a valuable letter of recommendation.
She's hard but fair and follows the school policy. I had family issues during my first term and she worked with me. She's big on communication.

Watson was supportive during my term with her and accommodating for my medical issues. However, I had to remind her to grade my work which I hadn't had to do with any other teacher. She didn't give the best answers to people who asked questions in lectures. Not the worst but not the best.
She is a tough grader and does not give clear feedback. She would dock points for me doing the same things I did for my higher level classes that I would not get docked for.

Harsher grader than most other PG psych instructors. For example, she lets seminars get way off topic then docks points for not participating in the off topic side conversations. She is not receptive to discussion or comments about this.
Very clear on feedbacks and overall great value on her experience, such a great energy.

She teaches at CSU Global; avoid her at all costs for your mental health!

Tried to give her the benefit of the doubt and ignore these reviews but i honestly had an awful semester. Anxiety through the roof the entire time. Felt like i had whiplash whenever a grade was posted because the grading was never consistent. Try to avoid if you care about your mental health

This professor has been terrible throughout the small block I'm required to take with her. She is belittling and you feel as though you cannot do anything to please her. She left several messages on the discussion forum that are definitely more of a 1 on 1 discussion, which made me lose all respect for them. Accuses of cheating every assignment.

Was an all around terrible professor. She makes you think she is nice with her little class introduction video but beware she is an awful professor. IF YOU HAVE HER MAKE SURE THAT LIFE DOES NOT HAPPEN TO YOU AS YOU WILL NOT BE ABLE TO MAKE UP ANYTHING PAST 2 WEEKS!

Glad to be out of that class.

Her grading is all over the place and nothing makes sense. I literally was unable to get anything higher than a B on any of my assignments or discussion boards. Hundred percent doesn't make any sense and she really showed no concern for any of the students.
Her grading is always late and doesn't follow the rubric.  She doesn't explain any of her grading, so you have no idea what you did wrong.
grading for no reason. no explanation. hard to reach. run!!!

review_highlights.comment: I really liked Prof Lagerwall. He was available for questions and provided great feedback when I needed it. I would retake him any day, and he was very knowledgeable about Criminal Law.

Professor Lagerwall was one of the most positive and productive instructors I have come across thus far. The seminars were highly engaging; he required participation in the form of comments/polls. He expects you to break down and analyze the elements of the assignments and does not encourage lazy work or slackers.

Put in the work to get an A

Make sure if you take this class that you meet ALL requirements asked and do extra! If you are short on words for assignments, even one word, he will fail you! He does not follow the grading rubric so it is almost a guessing game. His feedback does not follow instructions, he typically makes up his own requirements and will grade you based on that.

Make sure you can actually attend the seminars because he will require at least two pages for the option 2 reflection. It seems a little unfair. I wouldnt recommend him as a professor. Pick someone else.

She doesn't follow her own rubric and will assign you a 4 page paper that needs to be at least 7 pages to do what she actually wants. Pass

Professor Lackey is a very strict professor. She has a tendency to expand outside of the rubric when it comes to grading instead of following it exactly. She is a stickler for grammar and mechanics of papers and discussion posts. Seminars are engaging, as she has prompts that must be followed for credit. Overall, I didn't enjoy having her.

I have NEVER disliked a professor more. She is disrespectful, picks favorites, and she expects every paper to be written exactly like her. If you have an I-O degree path buckle up because you will have her as your professor at least 3 times. I dislike Purdue Global because of her. Good luck!

You get nothing out of lectures. Grading is extremely subjective and based on the way she would write. If you don't write it exactly as she would, or she doesn't think the standpoint is worthy, so many points taken off. They will probably take both student evaluation comments down for confirming that favorites are played (if it's true, it's true).
Great feedback, engaging and supportive.

analysis.name: Monal Patel
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: One of the better adjuncts in PG's EdD program (most are poor quality). The best seminars because she actually teaches, engages, and uses real-life examples (others don't). Authentic, cites sources in her insightful & instructive discussions. Friendly and helpful. Use Recite Works to help you avoid tiny APA errors that will cost you big points.

This instructor was very tough about proper use of APA and I was marked down many times for small mistakes here and there. That said, you better believe I learned it well! Other than that, she was informative, interesting, and understanding of the fact that we are doctoral students with jobs and families and lives. Extensions were always given.

By far the most personable professor I've had. She gives good feedback and is approachable. Seminars included actual instruction, discourse on topics related to content, and tips for assignments. I recommend attending. Dr. Patel is a true educator - she knows learning is a process and helps students along the way.

This professor nitpicks over every little thing. Even when I am using citations created by them, they constantly take off points for my citations being incorrect. The feedback given seems contradictory to the feedback they had given me earlier in the term. This professor seems to not know what their own expectations are so they make them up.

She is somewhat of a strict grader. She deducts points for APA format and is super strict about that. I have never received deductions for APA in any of my other courses before, but now I have. I find it strange because when I showed my assignments to other professors, they said they looked good and didn't understand why points are being taken off.

PLEASE PLEASE do not take this professor. She's extremely manipulative, annoying, and cannot speak in English clearly.

I never stop and give a rating like this but I feel that Dr. Mulleneaux had a great impact in my life. I'm happy I took her class. She gave us the real deal. Worst professor I have ever had in all my years of education. Literally makes what can be a fun class as miserable as possible. Seems to have erased the past reviews or else the rating would be even lower. Take anyone but her if you want to have a joyful schooling experience.

Prof. D is the Best! She treats you as an individual and is always so positive and encouraging. She understands that life happens and will always work with you. Single-handedly, one of the best professors I've ever had. Her philosophy is that "learning shouldn't be stressful". And that is an important philosophy in all of her classes. She is an amazing professor, and everything that she does, and her attitude for courses are done so you can to succeed.

This class opened up my mind to understanding what healthcare is and what it could be. Her class is definitely one I would recommend to my peers and Dr. D helps make it a pleasure to take.

analysis.name: Matthew  Route
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment:
This course genuinely seems to be all over the place. I genuinely dislike that Dr. Route expected you to teach yourself the material before he actually "teaches" it. The homework is never relevant
to the current unit but the next unit (hence why you have to teach yourself the content). There is not enough explanation for the math problems.

Professor Route is very clear and straightforward on the concepts and problems of physics, and was easy to follow. He is open to students' questions and is able to relate the topics
to real life situations, making it easier to understand. I also saw him during office hours (via Zoom) and he was pretty helpful when I was stuck on a problem.

He explains really well how the concepts that we learn about in the textbook can be applied to the real world and he makes sure that those examples are simple enough and really explain the concept. He does a really good job teaching the lecture and explaining any of the participation questions to make sure that students understand the information.

There is no practice exams given, just a ton of formulas that he never taught you how to use. Lectures only have the basic formulas, but he expects you to just know how to manipulate them. Exams were extremely difficult and were the only reason I got a C in this course. All my other categories were 95% and above. There is no curve for this course.

Not good at teaching physics. Does not explain anything and just reads off the slides. Exam averages are 50% at best and are not curved. He never seems to know what is going on with the course in general and cannot answer questions students have. The slides are also never up to date on Brightspace, and everything I learned, I taught myself.

analysis.name: Tyler Sorg
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He is an amazing teacher with great energy who presents the material as if it was a casual conversation. Not a tough grader at all and definitely gives good and visual feedback on assignments. Was my first teacher starting at Purdue and set the tone for a great experience. I will be graduating soon and am grateful for him setting the tone!

This professor is clearly very passionate about his job. He's incredibly caring and helpful! Has amazing lectures and lots of interactions with students. He's always been quickly available whenever I've needed him. He's made this class so fun, it was easy for me to be successful in it. I'm so thankful I had him this term!!
He is a very understanding teacher and is not strict about deadlines as long as you just get all of your work in. He is super funny and loves to chat in seminars. Had a great time in this class!

analysis.name: Sait Tarhan
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great Professor and very knowledgable. His grading is fair and consistent. Be mindful there is no sympathy for formatting errors.  Very engaging in Seminars and responsive for additional questions. Posts grades by Saturday following former week's conclusion. Would request for additional courses if available.

Gives a lot of additional info and resources in discussion boards and emails. As long as you put in the effort and double check your spelling and grammar he is in an easy grader. Keeps seminars fun as long as you're paying attention.
Great professor, I'm glad I got him for one of my first classes in college.

His seminars are engaging, and the time seems to move too quickly each week! He is always providing answers to questions, even if they are not related to the actual topic in the weekly seminar. He is genuinely one of the best professors I've ever had!

One of the best Professors at PGU. Seminars are a blast and there is so much information to go over. Very critical with grading assignments so check and check again your grammar and correct spelling. If I could score a 10 I would. Very informative and is easy to communicate with.

analysis.name: Karen Savarese
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I actually had Karen Savarese for two classes, PS124 and PS330 (neither is on the dropdown menu), and I wish she taught everything!
Professed Savrese is amazing I loved taking PS220 with her

I had Professor Savarese during my first term at Purdue Global. She really made me love that I'm a psychology major! Every seminar was so engaging, and she really made the material easy to understand. Very understanding of personal problems coming up as long as you reach out to her and let her know. I'd definitely love to work with her again!

analysis.name: Tedd Wheeler
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Wheeler has created a practical, real-life applicable business strategy course at Purdue Global. He uses Business Briefs instead of traditional APA style assignments with resources that we can actually use in real-world business scenarios. He clearly stated all assignment requirements and expectations and was consistently encouraging.

In my opinion, the professor was an excellent teacher. He thoroughly covered all topics and patiently explained concepts in terms we could understand. He helped me identify my writing weaknesses and provided valuable advice for improvement. Whenever I had questions outside of our seminar, I could email him, and he would reply within the day.

Professor Wheeler is very clear, organized, and supportive. He gives detailed feedback that really helps me understand the material and improve my work. I like how responsive he is to questions and how he makes the online class feel engaging. His teaching style keeps things straightforward and easy to follow, which makes learning less stressful

This professor is amazing. He makes the assignments easy to understand and offers assistance for anything you need help on. If I had any more classes to take I would definitely try to take one of his classes again. Great teacher.
Very good at what he does. Easy to learn and if you follow the process you will do good in his class. Very understanding and willing to work with you the best he can. Loved his class.

analysis.name: Mark Wilcox
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very knowledgeable about the content he teaches. Strict grader but very helpful and responsive if you have questions about anything. I learned a lot from his class. I'm an older learner and he reminds me of professors I had when I first started college; he cares about his students but wants them to learn.
He's a tough grader and absolutely full of himself. He will take absolutely every chance he can during seminar to talk about himself. He grades harshly if you make minor grammar or spelling mistakes.
He is a tough grader and does not seem to care about his students.

I thought he taught well and was specific in what he expected in homework and papers. I found the course relatively easy as long as you took the work seriously. It was a lot of time intensive papers, but the pay off was worth it retrospectively.
Though his class was a difficult one he was always reachable when i had questions and he would re-explain what ever you need until you understand it.

analysis.name: Dorothy Williams
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great teacher! Teaches in a very easy to understand way. Works through problems thoroughly and answers all questions. Responds very quickly to any questions, very understanding of any circumstances, gives plenty of time to complete assignments.

I absolutely love Professor Williams! She started class off by saying "you all start with an A (100%) what you choose to do with it is your choice." Following the grading rubric and attending seminar is the easiest way to get an amazing grade! I would absolutely love to work with this professor again!
Professor Williams did a good job keeping conversations exciting during seminar and gave consistent and timely feedback.

I did this class via online. She is a sweet, nice lady. Her class is pretty easy and she gives great feedback. I recommend everyone to take CM410. Her class is easier than lower level classed at Purdue Global.
One of the warmest and brightest auras a teacher has had. Simply logging in and seeing her smiling puts me in such a better mood.

analysis.name: David  White
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. White gets a bad rap but he's genuinely a good professor. Makes you better every week. He's a tough grader- and especially tough on citations and APA guidelines. However, he also is easy to reach during the week and is willing to make accommodations when life gets in the way of your studies. He's a true professor where you come out better.
This professor only cares about citing your paper. He never mentions anything about the content of the paper, only cites. Be prepared because this guy is tough and I hope I never encounter him again.
I wish not ever to have him again!
